content.text: Azure Lighthouse enables a managing tenant (an MSP, a central IT team, or an enterprise shared services group) to access and govern resources in customer tenants without requiring guest accounts or tenant switching. The mechanism is a cross-tenant delegation: the customer deploys an ARM registration definition that names the managing tenant and grants specific Azure RBAC roles to principals (users, groups, service principals) in that managing tenant. Once the delegation is active, the MSP principals' Entra ID tokens — issued by their own tenant — are accepted by the customer tenant's ARM, subject to the delegated role assignments.

attrs.alt: Azure Lighthouse — Managing Tenant (MSP) and Customer Tenant delegation flow

content.text: Lighthouse delegations are NOT guest accounts. No B2B invitation is sent. The customer tenant's Entra ID does not hold the MSP user's identity. The trust is at the ARM layer via the registration definition resource — an ARM token from the managing tenant is valid for the delegated scope in the customer tenant as long as the delegation exists.
Yes — MSP tenant ID stamped in activity log

A Lighthouse delegation is created by deploying a microsoft.managedServices/registrationDefinitions resource at subscription scope in the customer tenant. The definition names the managing tenant and lists authorizations: each authorization maps a principal in the managing tenant to an Azure RBAC role in the customer scope.

# --- Customer-side: deploy the delegation template (run in customer tenant) ---
# Customer logs in to THEIR OWN tenant and deploys the ARM template at subscription scope

az login --tenant $CUSTOMER_TENANT_ID

az deployment sub create   --location eastus   --template-file ./lighthouse-delegation.json   --parameters mspTenantId=$MSP_TENANT_ID                mspOfferName="Contoso MSP Managed Services"

# Verify registration definition was created
az managedservices definition list --query   "[].{name:properties.registrationDefinitionName, managed:properties.managedByTenantId}" -o table

# Verify assignment (activates the delegation)
az managedservices assignment list

# --- MSP-side: access customer resources using --tenant flag ---
az login  # MSP engineer logs in to MSP tenant (Tenant A)

# List resources in customer subscription (customer's tenant injected via --tenant)
az resource list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUBSCRIPTION_ID   --tenant $CUSTOMER_TENANT_ID   --query "[?type=='Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines'].{name:name, rg:resourceGroup}"

# Run a command in customer RG (contributor role)
az vm stop   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUBSCRIPTION_ID   --resource-group prod-rg   --name customer-vm-01

# Update delegation: add a new authorization
# Re-deploy the ARM template with updated authorizations[] array.
# Lighthouse does an in-place update (same GUID for definition name = update, not new resource).

# Remove delegation entirely (customer does this to revoke MSP access)
ASSIGNMENT_ID=$(az managedservices assignment list --query "[0].name" -o tsv)
DEFINITION_ID=$(az managedservices definition list --query "[0].name" -o tsv)

az managedservices assignment delete --assignment $ASSIGNMENT_ID
az managedservices definition delete --definition $DEFINITION_ID

Azure Lighthouse supports eligible authorizations using Privileged Identity Management (PIM). Instead of granting permanent high-privilege roles to MSP engineers, eligible authorizations require just-in-time activation — the MSP engineer activates their assignment for a defined duration and justification, then the elevated access expires automatically.

# MSP engineer activates JIT eligible role (runs in MSP tenant portal or CLI)
# CLI: az role assignment activation create (requires az pim extension)
az extension add --name pim

# List eligible assignments for the MSP engineer across delegated scopes
az pim eligible-assignment list   --assignee $(az ad signed-in-user show --query id -o tsv)   --include-groups true   --query "[].{scope:properties.scope, role:properties.expandedProperties.roleDefinition.displayName}"

# Activate contributor role for 4 hours with justification
az pim assignment activate   --id <ELIGIBLE_ASSIGNMENT_ID>   --justification "Incident INC-20240315: disk full on customer-vm-01"   --duration PT4H

From the managing tenant, Lighthouse enables at-scale operations across all delegated customer subscriptions. Azure Monitor queries, Azure Policy compliance views, and Azure Advisor recommendations all support cross-tenant views — the MSP can view and act without switching directories.

# --- From the MSP tenant: list all delegated subscriptions ---
az managedservices definition list --query   "[].{customer:properties.registrationDefinitionName, managed:properties.managedByTenantId}"

# List all VMs across ALL customer subscriptions (requires contributor+ on each)
# Use Azure Resource Graph cross-subscription query (runs in MSP tenant context)
az graph query -q "
Resources
| where type == 'microsoft.compute/virtualmachines'
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location
" --subscriptions $CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID $CUSTOMER_SUB2_ID $CUSTOMER_SUB3_ID

# Assign an Azure Policy definition in a customer subscription (from MSP tenant)
# Requires Policy Contributor role delegation
az policy assignment create   --name "require-tags-prod"   --display-name "Require environment tag on all resources"   --policy "1e30110a-5ceb-460c-a204-c1c3969c6d62"   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID   --params '{"tagName":{"value":"environment"}}'

# Check policy compliance across customer sub (from MSP tenant)
az policy state list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID   --filter "complianceState eq 'NonCompliant'"   --query "[].{resource:resourceId, policy:policyDefinitionName}"   -o table | head -20

# Delegate Azure Monitor: query customer Log Analytics from MSP tenant
# (requires Log Analytics Reader role delegation)
az monitor log-analytics query   --workspace $CUSTOMER_LA_WORKSPACE_ID   --analytics-query "Heartbeat | summarize LastCall=max(TimeGenerated) by Computer | order by LastCall asc"   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID
Audit: Activity Log for Delegated Actions

Every action taken by an MSP engineer through a Lighthouse delegation is recorded in the customer tenant's Azure Activity Log. The log entry includes the MSP engineer's UPN, the MSP tenant ID, and the customer resource targeted. This provides the customer with complete accountability for all delegated operations.

# View Activity Log in the customer tenant for Lighthouse-delegated operations
# (Customer runs this — or MSP with sufficient scope)
az monitor activity-log list   --subscription $CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID   --caller ""   --query "[?claims."http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences"].    {time:eventTimestamp, caller:caller, operation:operationName.localizedValue,      resource:resourceId, tenantId:tenantId}"   --start-time "2024-03-15T00:00:00Z"   -o table | head -30

# KQL query to find all Lighthouse-delegated operations (run in customer tenant LA)
# AzureActivity
# | where HomeTenantId != TenantId           // cross-tenant action
# | project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerHomeTenantId=HomeTenantId,
#           OperationName, ResourceGroup, Resource, ActivityStatusValue
# | where ActivityStatusValue == "Success"
# | order by TimeGenerated desc

# Alert on Lighthouse actions that modify security-sensitive resources
# (customer creates this in their own tenant)
# AzureActivity
# | where HomeTenantId != TenantId
# | where OperationName contains "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments"
#     or OperationName contains "Microsoft.Security/policies"
#     or OperationName contains "Microsoft.KeyVault"
# | project TimeGenerated, Caller, CallerHomeTenantId, OperationName, Resource

# Lighthouse audit via ARM API (customer lists all registration assignments)
az rest   --method GET   --uri "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/$CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID/providers/Microsoft.ManagedServices/registrationAssignments?api-version=2022-10-01&\$expand=registrationDefinition"   --query "value[].{name:name,             mspTenant:properties.registrationDefinition.properties.managedByTenantId,             offer:properties.registrationDefinition.properties.registrationDefinitionName}"
Registration definition alone grants nothing; always deploy the registrationAssignment too
Owner and User Access Administrator are not allowed as Lighthouse authorizations — use Contributor + specific sub-roles
principalId points to a user (not group)
Use Entra ID security groups; add/remove individuals from the group, not from the delegation
Missing Policy Contributor for policy enforcement
az policy assignment create fails with 403
Add roleDefinitionId for Resource Policy Contributor (36243c78…) to authorizations
Customer deployed delegation at RG scope but MSP needs subscription scope
Cross-sub Resource Graph queries return no results
Redeploy delegation at subscription scope or add additional RG-scope delegations
Set multiFactorAuthProvider to Azure in eligibleAuthorizations JIT policy

MSPs with many customers can publish a Lighthouse Managed Service Offer on the Azure Marketplace. The customer accepts the offer from the portal, which automatically deploys the registration definition without the customer needing to manage ARM templates directly. For enterprise use, ARM template deployment through Azure DevOps pipelines scales across dozens of customer subscriptions.

# --- MSP: publish offer via Partner Center (programmatic prep) ---
# 1. Build the offer package: authorization JSON + offer metadata
# Partner Center → Cloud Partner Portal → Managed Services → New Offer

# The authorizations JSON for the offer is identical in structure to the ARM template's
# authorizations array — same principalId/roleDefinitionId/principalIdDisplayName fields.

# --- Enterprise: deploy delegation to multiple subscriptions via pipeline ---
CUSTOMER_SUBS=(
  "$CUSTOMER_SUB1_ID"
  "$CUSTOMER_SUB2_ID"
  "$CUSTOMER_SUB3_ID"
)

for SUB in "${CUSTOMER_SUBS[@]}"; do
  echo "Deploying Lighthouse delegation to subscription $SUB"
  az deployment sub create     --subscription "$SUB"     --location eastus     --template-file ./lighthouse-delegation.json     --parameters       mspTenantId="$MSP_TENANT_ID"       mspOfferName="Contoso MSP v2"     --no-wait
done

# Wait for all deployments
for SUB in "${CUSTOMER_SUBS[@]}"; do
  az deployment sub wait --subscription "$SUB" --name "lighthouse-delegation" --created
done

# Verify all delegations are active
for SUB in "${CUSTOMER_SUBS[@]}"; do
  echo "=== $SUB ==="
  az managedservices assignment list --subscription "$SUB"     --query "[].{name:name, provisioningState:properties.provisioningState}" -o table
done

# --- MSP: consolidated view of all delegated customers (run in MSP tenant) ---
az managedservices definition list   --query "[].{offer:properties.registrationDefinitionName,                msp:properties.managedByTenantId,                authorizations:length(properties.authorizations)}" -o table

// All delegated subscriptions visible to the MSP (run in MSP tenant portal)
// (No CLI equivalent — use Azure Resource Graph Explorer in the portal)

// Count resources per customer subscription
Resources
| summarize resourceCount=count() by subscriptionId
| join kind=inner (ResourceContainers
  | where type == "microsoft.resources/subscriptions"
  | project subscriptionId, subscriptionName=name) on subscriptionId
| project subscriptionName, subscriptionId, resourceCount
| order by resourceCount desc

// VMs not running latest OS image across all delegated subscriptions
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.compute/virtualmachines"
| extend osVersion = tostring(properties.storageProfile.imageReference.sku)
| where osVersion !contains "2022" and osVersion !contains "20_04" and osVersion !contains "22_04"
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, osVersion

// NSGs with Any/Any rules across all delegated subscriptions
Resources
| where type == "microsoft.network/networksecuritygroups"
| mv-expand rule = properties.securityRules
| where rule.properties.sourceAddressPrefix == "*"
    and rule.properties.destinationAddressPrefix == "*"
    and rule.properties.access == "Allow"
| project name, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, ruleName=rule.name

// Policy non-compliance summary across all customer subscriptions
PolicyResources
| where type == "microsoft.policyinsights/policystates"
| where properties.complianceState == "NonCompliant"
| summarize nonCompliantCount=count() by subscriptionId, policyDefinitionName=tostring(properties.policyDefinitionName)
| order by nonCompliantCount desc

Azure Lighthouse delegated resource management allows an MSP or central IT team to access and govern customer tenant resources using their own Entra ID identities — no guest accounts, no directory switching. The customer deploys a microsoft.managedServices/registrationDefinitions ARM resource naming the managing tenant and the RBAC roles granted. Eligible authorizations with PIM enforce just-in-time activation and MFA for high-privilege roles. All MSP actions appear in the customer Activity Log stamped with the MSP tenant ID. At-scale features (cross-tenant Resource Graph, Policy, Monitor) let the MSP operate across hundreds of customers from a single portal session.
